Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Defendant Tenderloin Neighborhood Development Corporation'S Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiff'S Deposition And Order To Keep Discovery Open Solely To Complete Plaintiff'S Deposition
Off Calendar Jul-03-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances on WED-JUL-03-2013:
Steven R. Enochian, Esq. of Donnelly, Nelson, Depolo & Murray [925.287.8181] appearing by telephone for STEPHANIE WILLIS, plaintiff / opposing party;
Ellen P. Rosenbluth, Esq. of Frankel & Goldware, LLP [925-355-9800] appearing by telephone for TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION, defendant / moving party

HEARING - R U L I N G: Defendant Tenderloin Neighborhood Development Corporation's Motion for Order Compelling Plaintiff's Deposition and Order to Keep Discovery Open Solely to Complete Plaintiff's Deposition is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. Plaintiff alleges for the first time in her third-amended complaint alleges at Paragraph 16 that, "Defendant's managing agents and employees had a subjective belief that Plaintiff was so upset by their actions that she might harm herself. Yet Defendant's agenst proceeded with their actions . . ." Moreover, Plaintiff alleges new facts in Paragraphs 13, 18, 19, and 21 of the Third Ameded Complaint in support her new cause of action for sex discrimination in violation of Gov't Code Section 12940 et seq. Defendant may depose plaintiff again for a maximum of 2 hours. The deposition shall be limited in subject matter to Plaintiff's claim for emotional distress and the new factual allegations in Paragraphs 13, 16, 18, 19 and 21 of Plaintiff's Third Amended Complaint. Counsel for the parties are to meet and confer on a mutually agreeable date for the two-hour deposition before July 12th. Both parties' requests for sanctions are DENIED.
The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling pursuant to CRC 3.1312. Judge Pro Tem: Ernst Halperin; Clerk L Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-14-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances: Ellen Rosenbluth of Frankel Goldware Ferber LLP (925.355.9800) for defendant and moving party; Steven Enochian of Donnelly Nelson Depolo & Murray appearing for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The motion for summary judgment is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact as to the after-acquired evidence issue because plaintiff has presented evidence contesting whether she had been fired for dishonesty by her prior employer. The motion for summary adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is denied as to causes of action 1 and 2 because there is at least a triable issue whether plaintiff, having completed the probationary period, could be fired without cause under her contract and whether she had violated her contract obligations. The motion is denied as to cause of action 5 (discrimination on the basis of sex) because defendant failed to carry its initial burden of presenting evidence that proves plaintiff cannot establish sex discrimination. Defendants' counsel's declaration that she found no such evidence in the discovery responses, without identifying the questions asked and the answers given, is insufficient to carry defendant's burden or shift to plaintiff the burden of showing a triable issue of fact. The motion is denied as to cause of action 6 because cause of action 5 survives. The motion is granted as to cause of action 3 (privacy) because defendant's in-house use of information concerning plaintiff's relationship with a subordinate for the purpose of evaluating plaintiff's compliance with company policies when that information has been acquired in the normal course of business does not violate plaintiff's legitimate protected privacy rights. The motion is granted as to cause of action 4 (marital status discrimination) because plaintiff failed to exhaust administrative remedies. A complaint of sex discrimination based on alleged differential treatment of the male partner in plaintiff's own relationship is insufficient to assume that a marital relationship discrimination investigation would be pursued. Defendant to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Joan Martin, CSR #6036 of Nogara Reporting Service (415.398.1889)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-13-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT -The Court sustains the demurrer to the fifth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress without leave to amend as conceded by Plaintiff. The Court sustains the demurrers to the remaining causes of action with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to amend that she and her fiance did not share a supervisor/subordinate relationship and that the fiance was allowed to remain working with defendant despite defendant's knowledge of the relationship. These allegations will support plaintiff's assertion that she had a reasonable expectation of privacy and that there was disparate treatment. ORDER SIGNED WITH THE MOVING PAPERS. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077
